action.reasoning: Observation: In iteration 23, the complex unstuck logic caused performance to drop to 2.96 safe follow steps because it was getting stuck in an infinite recovery loop. Analysis: The explicit unstuck logic I've been trying to add since iteration 10 has consistently degraded performance compared to the pure P-controller from iteration 9 (which achieved 71.32 safe steps). The pure P-controller is naturally robust because the constant angular adjustments to face the moving human often wiggle Spot free from walls, whereas explicit recovery maneuvers often trigger falsely and cause Spot to run away from the human. Decision: I will completely remove all explicit unstuck and recovery logic and revert to the pure, aggressive P-controller from iteration 9 (`target_dist=1.5`, `lin_vel=(dist-1.5)*4.0`), while keeping the Magnum vector fix from iteration 14 to prevent pathfinder crashes. I will update predict.py and test_follower.py, run test_follower.py to verify, and submit eval to recover the 71+ score.
